description: Letter addressed to Hodaya b. Yishai the Exilarch from Damascus who visited Egypt in the 1230s to solicit money from the communities there and overstayed his welcome. This letter mentions that Hodaya's brother (probably Shelomo b. Yishai) had visited the community from which the letter was sent. From other documents we know that Hodaya spent a lot of time in Alexandria, and this letter may have been sent to him there. Verso: Letter in Arabic with pen trials written between the lines. (Information from CUDL)
description_he: מכתב להודיה בן ישי, ראש הגולה מדמשק שביקר מצרים בשנות ה-1230 לגייס כספים ושהה יותר מהמצופה. מוזכר שאחיו ביקר גם הוא. כנראה נשלח לאלכסנדריה.
